goal: Develop practical language skills for travel, everyday conversation, and enjoying media without subtitles
Be travel-ready in Spanish, able to navigate basic interactions (getting around, ordering food, small talk) within six months.
constraints: Prioritize languages with practical near-term utility and real-world applicability
Timeline constraint: six months to achieve travel-ready Spanish.
Online speaking practice will be the primary mode due to limited local native speakers.
decisions: Draft a concrete week-by-week plan for a 12-week period (weekday morning 40–50 min sessions + Sunday block) with specific resources and a suggestion for when to slot a weekly tutor session.
Create first set of contextual sentence cards (8–10 travel-focused sentences for Anki import)
open: Assess user's current language experience or proficiency (if any)
Identify preferred learning resources and formats (apps, textbooks, tutors, classes, media)
Draft a concrete 3–6 month plan once user provides answers to questions
Apply practicality filter to language recommendations (focus on near-term usefulness, travel viability, and community availability)
Provide two very specific habit-stack examples tailored to the user's routine once they share a routine (e.g., after coffee, during commute)
Incorporate a learn-by-doing approach (conversational practice and audio) into the plan based on user preference for doing over grammar drills)
Confirm online-only speaking practice approach and list preferred platforms (e.g., italki, Tandem, HelloTalk, Meetup online groups) and schedule
